arguments.query_context: A backend engineering team is planning a database migration from PostgreSQL 12 to PostgreSQL 15. The migration involves schema changes, data transformations, index rebuilds, and application code updates. The team has already done a thorough analysis of each component. Time estimates are in hours. The migration will be performed during a maintenance window. The team has 4 engineers available.

previous_task_results.result: Schema changes: 3 hours. This includes adding 5 new columns, renaming 3 tables, dropping 2 deprecated views, and adding NOT NULL constraints with backfill.
Data transformation: 8 hours. The largest table has 50M rows and requires a full-table scan for UUID normalization. Estimated at 2 hours per 10M rows on current hardware.
Index rebuilds: 4 hours. 23 indexes across 8 tables need to be rebuilt. Concurrent index creation will be used to minimize locking. The two largest indexes are on the events and audit_log tables.
Application code: 6 hours. 3 services need connection string updates, 2 services need query adjustments for PG15 syntax changes, and all services need integration testing.
Rollback preparation: 2 hours. Logical replication slot setup, snapshot verification, and runbook documentation.

previous_subtask_results.result: Schema migration and rollback prep can run in parallel (saves 2 hours). Data transformation must run sequentially after schema changes.
Critical path: schema(3h) + data transformation(8h) + index rebuild(4h) = 15h sequential minimum. App updates can run in parallel with index rebuild.

arguments.subtask: Based on all the gathered information, what is the estimated total time to complete the migration?

scoring_criteria: final should be around 15 hours (critical path). Accepting 13-17 hours range due to parallelization assumptions. Response must be valid JSON. Confidence should reflect some uncertainty.

arguments.query_context: A startup is preparing to launch their SaaS product in 2 weeks. The engineering team has completed a thorough pre-launch audit covering security, performance, reliability, and compliance. The product handles sensitive financial data for SMB customers. The team needs to prioritize which issues to fix immediately vs. defer to post-launch. Critical issues are those that could cause data loss, security breaches, regulatory non-compliance, or major outages. The team has limited engineering bandwidth for the next 2 weeks.

previous_task_results.result: Issues found: (1) API endpoints missing rate limiting - could allow brute force attacks. (2) User passwords stored with MD5 hash - must upgrade to bcrypt. (3) SQL queries in legacy module use string concatenation - potential SQL injection. (4) JWT tokens have no expiry set - tokens valid indefinitely. (5) Sensitive PII logged in application logs - GDPR violation risk. (6) CORS policy allows all origins in production config.

Issues found: (1) N+1 query problem in dashboard endpoint - loads 500+ queries for large accounts. (2) No database connection pooling configured - will exhaust connections under load. (3) Large file uploads block main thread - no async processing. (4) No CDN configured for static assets - slow load times for international users. (5) Database missing indexes on frequently queried columns (user_id, created_at, status).

Issues found: (1) No health check endpoints configured - load balancer cannot detect unhealthy instances. (2) Background job queue has no retry logic - failed jobs are silently dropped. (3) No circuit breaker for external payment API calls - cascading failures possible. (4) Database backups not tested for restore capability. (5) No alerting configured for error rate spikes.

Issues found: (1) No data retention policy implemented - violates GDPR Article 5. (2) No audit log for sensitive data access - required for SOC2. (3) User data deletion flow incomplete - GDPR right to erasure not fully implemented. (4) Privacy policy does not reflect actual data collection practices. (5) No DPA (Data Processing Agreement) with third-party processors.

previous_subtask_results.result: Critical: MD5 passwords (immediate breach risk), SQL injection (data theft risk), JWT no expiry (session hijack risk). High: Rate limiting, PII logging, CORS.

previous_subtask_results.subtask: Rank compliance issues by launch-blocking risk
previous_subtask_results.result: Launch-blocking: MD5 passwords violates security standards, PII in logs violates GDPR, incomplete data deletion violates GDPR right to erasure. These could result in immediate regulatory action.

previous_subtask_results.subtask: Assess performance issues for launch day impact
previous_subtask_results.result: The N+1 query and missing connection pooling are most likely to cause outages on launch day with real traffic. Database indexes are also high priority but can be added with minimal downtime.

arguments.subtask: Identify the top 3 most critical issues that need to be addressed before the production launch.

expected.final: MD5 password hashing, SQL injection vulnerability, PII in logs (GDPR violation)

scoring_criteria: final must identify at least 2 of these 3 critical issues: (1) MD5/weak password hashing, (2) SQL injection, (3) PII in logs / GDPR violation. Exact wording doesn't matter. Response MUST be valid JSON with all three fields (reasoning, final, confidence). This is the key test case for the long-input JSON format issue.
